audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for wedding feast parable, garment warning, Caesar tax question, Sadducees/resurrection, greatest command, and Messiah/Son of David question.

verses.restored: Jesus answered again in parables, saying to them:
The reign of the heavens is like a human king who made a wedding feast for his son.
He sent his servants to call those invited to the wedding feast, but they were not willing to come.
Again he sent other servants, saying, Tell those invited: Look, my meal is prepared; my oxen and fattened animals are sacrificed, and everything is ready. Come to the wedding feast.
But they paid no attention and went away, one to his own field, another to his trade.
The rest seized his servants, mistreated them, and killed them.
The king was enraged; he sent his armies, destroyed those murderers, and burned their city.
Then he said to his servants, The wedding feast is ready, but those invited were not worthy.
Therefore go to the crossroads of the ways, and invite to the wedding feast as many as you find.
Those servants went out into the ways and gathered all whom they found, both harmful and good; and the wedding hall was filled with guests.
When the king entered to behold the guests, he saw there a person not wearing a wedding garment.
He said to him, Friend, how did you enter here without a wedding garment? But he was speechless.
Then the king said to the servants, Bind his feet and hands and cast him into the outer darkness; there will be weeping and grinding of teeth.
For many are called, but few are chosen.
Then the Pharisees went and took counsel how they might trap him in word.

They sent their disciples to him with the Herodians, saying, Teacher, we know you are true and teach the way of the Divine in truth, and you do not care about anyone's status, for you do not look at people's face.
Tell us, then, what do you think? Is it lawful to give tribute to Caesar, or not?
Knowing their harmful intent, Jesus said, Why do you test me, hypocrites?
Show me the coin of the tribute. They brought him a denarius.
He said to them, Whose image and inscription is this?
They said, Caesar's. Then he said to them, Give back, therefore, the things of Caesar to Caesar, and the things of the Divine to the Divine.
Hearing this, they marveled; leaving him, they went away.
On that day Sadducees came to him, those saying there is no resurrection, and they questioned him,
saying, Teacher, Moses said, If someone dies having no children, his brother shall marry his wife and raise up seed for his brother.
There were seven brothers among us. The first married and died, and having no seed, left his wife to his brother.
Likewise the second and third, down to the seventh.
Last of all, the woman died.
In the resurrection, then, of which of the seven will she be wife? For they all had her.
Jesus answered them, You are wandering, not knowing the Scriptures nor the power of the Divine.
For in the resurrection they neither marry nor are given in marriage, but are like messengers in heaven.
Concerning the resurrection of the dead, have you not read what was spoken to you by the Divine, saying,
I am the Divine of Abraham, the Divine of Isaac, and the Divine of Jacob? He is not Divine of dead ones, but of living ones.
When the crowds heard, they were astonished at his teaching.
When the Pharisees heard that he had silenced the Sadducees, they gathered together.
One of them, a legal expert, asked, testing him:
Teacher, which command is great in the Law?
He said to him, You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul/life, and with all your mind.
This is the great and first command.
A second is like it: You shall love your neighbor as yourself.
On these two commands hang all the Law and the Prophets.
While the Pharisees were gathered, Jesus questioned them,
saying, What do you think about the Messiah? Whose son is he? They said to him, David's.
He said to them, How then does David in Spirit call him Lord, saying,
The Lord said to my Lord, Sit at my right hand until I put your enemies beneath your feet?
If David calls him Lord, how is he his son?
No one was able to answer him a word, nor from that day did anyone dare to question him further.
